docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Mod Purpose RED4ext Required by Codeware and Equipment-EX Redscript Scripting runtime Codeware Localization and extended scripting APIs Input Loader Custom keybind definitions Mod Settings In-game settings menu Equipment-EX Outfit management system

docs.title: Soft Requirements
docs.text:
These mods are not required, but unlock additional features. Without them, the relevant triggers never fire and the features silently do nothing.

Mod What it enables Immersive Rippers ripper outfit keyword — without this, V never sits in a ripperdoc chair Immersive Rippers - Dogtown Extends ripper support to Dogtown Hangout Romances Drive scene outfit handling Immersive Shower Eliminates the shower scene flash (see Known Issues)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Extract the archive
  2. Copy the r6 folder to your Cyberpunk 2077 directory
  3. Configure in-game: Settings → Mod Settings → Dynamic Wardrobe

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
{% hint style="warning" %} Always delete the old version before installing a new one. Leftover scripts from previous versions can cause conflicts and errors. CustomApartments.reds and ContextMap.reds are user-editable config files — back them up before deleting, as new versions will overwrite them.

  1. Back up Core/CustomApartments.reds and Core/ContextMap.reds if you've customized them
  2. Delete the r6/scripts/Dynamic Wardrobe folder and r6/input/DynamicWardrobeInput.xml
  3. Install the new version
  4. Restore your backed-up config files

docs.title: Uninstallation
docs.text: Delete the r6/scripts/Dynamic Wardrobe folder and r6/input/DynamicWardrobeInput.xml. See Save Data & Uninstall for details on what the mod writes to your save.

docs.title: Mod Settings
docs.text:
All settings can be adjusted in real-time through Settings → Mod Settings → Dynamic Wardrobe:

  • Master Toggle — completely enable/disable the mod without uninstalling
  • Show Location Key — configurable keybind (default: F10) to display the current location name
  • Shower Deferral — when enabled, the home outfit won't apply immediately on entering an apartment. Instead, it applies after the game naturally undresses V (shower, sleep, etc.)

docs.title: Custom Apartments
docs.text: Dynamic Wardrobe supports modded apartments. Add an apartment's entrance coordinates to Core/CustomApartments.reds and the mod handles the rest.

docs.title: Built-in (no setup needed)
docs.text: Apartment Location H10 (V's starting apartment) Little China Glen Apartment Glen Northside Apartment Northside Japantown Apartment Japantown Corpo Plaza Apartment Corpo Plaza Dogtown Hideout (Kress Street) Dogtown (Phantom Liberty) Judy's Apartment Kabuki (unlocked by quest)

docs.title: Pre-configured Custom Apartments
docs.text:
Apartment Location Unlock Condition River's Trailer Badlands Complete "Following the River" (romance or friendship)

River's apartment is included as a ready-to-use example in CustomApartments.reds.

docs.title: Adding Your Own
docs.text:
Open Core/CustomApartments.reds and add entries using the config API:

DWApartment.Add(apartments, true)
  .Entrance(-905.00, 1868.56, 42.37);

docs.title: Available Options
docs.text: Method What It Does DWApartment.Add(apartments, hasShower) Creates a new apartment. hasShower controls whether the shower deferral setting applies .Entrance(x, y, z) Adds a door location (use multiple times for apartments with more than one entrance) .Gate("fact_name", minValue) Adds a quest requirement — ALL gates must be met .OrGate("fact_name", minValue) Adds an alternative quest requirement — at least ONE must be met

docs.title: Example: Two entrances with a quest requirement
docs.text:
DWApartment.Add(apartments, true)
  .Entrance(-100.0, 200.0, 50.0)
  .Entrance(-102.0, 198.0, 50.0)
  .Gate("my_quest_fact", 1);

docs.title: Example: Multiple quest paths (either one unlocks it)
docs.text:
DWApartment.Add(apartments, false)
  .Entrance(300.0, -400.0, 10.0)
  .OrGate("quest_path_a", 1)
  .OrGate("quest_path_b", 1);

docs.title: hasShower
docs.text:
• true — the apartment has a shower. If shower deferral is turned on in mod settings, V won't change into the home outfit right away — it waits until after the shower
  • false — no shower. V changes into the home outfit immediately on entry

docs.title: Quest Gates
docs.text:
• .Gate() — AND logic. All gates must pass for the apartment to activate
  • .OrGate() — OR logic. At least one must pass. Use this for apartments that can be unlocked through different quest paths

docs.title: How It's Detected
docs.text:
The mod detects custom apartments by watching for a specific game state change (scene tier transition) while V is near the configured door coordinates (~1 meter). When V leaves, the mod detects the transition back to normal gameplay and switches to the appropriate outfit.

{% hint style="info" %} Why some locations won't work — Custom apartment detection relies on the game's scene tier system. When V walks through a door into an interior, the game transitions from Tier 1 (free roam) to Tier 2 (restricted movement) — that transition is what the mod listens for. Open-air locations like camps, tents, or rooftops never trigger this transition because there's no loading door, so the mod has no event to react to. This isn't something that can be worked around — the game simply doesn't signal these entries.

docs.title: Finding Coordinates
docs.text: The config file includes console commands for looking up entrance coordinates and quest facts using Cyber Engine Tweaks. Open the CET console in-game to use them.

docs.title: Combat & Danger Zones
docs.text: Dynamic Wardrobe automatically swaps to a combat outfit when V enters a fight or a hostile area, and puts the previous outfit back on when it's over.

docs.title: Fights
docs.text: When V enters combat, the mod swaps to a combat outfit. When the fight ends, the previous outfit comes back.

docs.title: Hostile Areas
docs.text: Gang hideouts, enemy territory, and other dangerous zones trigger combat outfits even before shots are fired. If V enters an actual fight while in a dangerous zone, the outfit stays — no double-swap. When V leaves the zone, the previous outfit is restored.

docs.title: Location-Aware Combat
docs.text:
Combine combat with location or region keywords for area-specific combat gear:

Outfit Name Where It Applies combat Anywhere (the default) combat street Only in street areas combat wild Only in wild/badlands areas combat badlands Only in Badlands specifically combat street club Only at street clubs (e.g. Afterlife)

docs.title: How It Picks
docs.text: combat + exact location → combat + region keywords → plain "combat"

docs.title: Good to Know
docs.text:
• Combat outfits are skipped at home and during ripperdoc sessions
  • Location swaps are paused during fights — V won't change into a location outfit mid-combat
  • If the previous outfit no longer exists when combat ends, the mod picks the best match for V's current location instead

docs.title: Quest Outfit Protection
docs.text: When the game forces V into a quest outfit (e.g. the diving suit), Dynamic Wardrobe steps back completely — no swaps of any kind until the quest outfit is removed.

docs.title: What This Mod Wraps
docs.text:
Dynamic Wardrobe hooks into the following game systems:

  • PlayerPuppet class — adds fields for outfit state tracking
  • Equipment System — wraps QuestDisableWardrobeSetRequest, QuestRestoreWardrobeSetRequest, UnequipWardrobeSet, OnUnequipRequest
  • Blackboard System — listens to UI_Map.currentLocationEnumName for location changes
  • SceneTierIVEvents — wraps OnEnter for ripperdoc detection and unequip blocking
  • WorkspotFinishedEvent — wraps for ripperdoc restore and block clearing
  • QuestsSystem — listens to a3_hangout_romances_running for drive scene end detection
  • PlayerPuppet.OnCombatStateChanged — wraps for combat outfit entry/exit
  • PlayerPuppet.OnZoneChange — wraps for dangerous zone detection
  • PlayerPuppet.OnSceneTierChange — wraps for scene tier location blocking

docs.title: High Risk — Will Definitely Conflict
docs.text:
{% hint style="danger" %} Other Equipment-EX automation mods — Any mod that also wraps QuestDisable/QuestRestore wardrobe requests or manages EX outfit loading will conflict. Both mods will fight over which outfit to apply during scene transitions.

Example: Apartment Outfits

{% hint style="danger" %} Mods that modify Equipment-EX's overlay system — Mods that call Deactivate/Activate on the OutfitSystem directly will conflict. Dynamic Wardrobe carefully manages EX's overlay state during scenes.

Symptoms: outfits not applying, EX overlay stuck in wrong state.

docs.title: Low Risk — Generally Compatible
docs.text:
• Equipment-EX itself — fully compatible. Dynamic Wardrobe is built on top of EX and uses its API properly. It only reads and loads outfits, never creates or modifies outfit data.
  • Appearance mods (AMM, custom appearances) — compatible. Dynamic Wardrobe only manages EX outfit sets, not individual items or appearances.
  • UI/HUD mods — fully compatible. No shared systems. The mod only shows occasional notifications via SimpleScreenMessage.
  • Other wardrobe/clothing mods — compatible unless they also automate Equipment-EX outfits.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Real-world outfit setups showing how Dynamic Wardrobe picks outfits in different scenarios.

docs.title: Basic Setup
docs.text:
V's outfits: home cozy, outdoor streetwear, nude, combat

  • Walk into the apartment → home cozy
  • Leave the apartment → outdoor streetwear
  • Get into a fight → combat, then back to outdoor streetwear when it's over
  • Take a shower at home → nude, then home cozy after

docs.title: Location-Specific Outfits
docs.text:
V's outfits: outdoor casual, afterlife, japantown formal

  • At the Afterlife → afterlife (exact match wins)
  • In Japantown → japantown formal (exact match wins)
  • Anywhere else → outdoor casual

docs.title: Region Keywords
docs.text:
V's outfits: outdoor, corpo elegant, street tough, club party

  • At Corpo Plaza → corpo elegant
  • In Kabuki → street tough
  • At the Afterlife → club party
  • In the Badlands → outdoor (no "wild" outfit, so the default kicks in)

docs.title: Mixing It All Together
docs.text:
V's outfits: outdoor, corpo casual, club fancy, corpo club vip, afterlife

  • At the Afterlife → afterlife (exact location always wins)
  • At Clouds (corpo + club area) → corpo club vip (2 keywords beat 1)
  • At Embers (corpo + club area) → corpo club vip (same match)
  • At Lizzie's Bar (street + club area) → club fancy ("corpo club vip" doesn't apply — not a corpo area)
  • At Corpo Plaza (corpo, no club) → corpo casual
  • In the Badlands → outdoor

docs.title: Multiple Outfits = Variety
docs.text:
V's outfits: home cozy, home towel, home lazy

  • Walk into the apartment → one of the three is picked at random
  • Leave and come back → might get a different one

docs.title: Combat + Location
docs.text:
V's outfits: combat, combat street, combat wild

  • Fight in Kabuki (street area) → combat street
  • Fight in the Badlands (wild area) → combat wild
  • Fight at Corpo Plaza → combat (no specific match, so the generic one applies)

docs.title: Shower Scene Flash
docs.text:
During shower scenes, there's a brief visual flash when switching outfits. This is a game engine limitation and can't be fixed by the mod.

{% hint style="success" %} Workaround — Use Immersive Shower — it undresses V before the shower starts. Since V is already nude when the transition happens, the flash becomes invisible. Works out of the box.

docs.title: Loading a Save Near Doors
docs.text:
The game simplifies location names when saving — for example, LittleChina_Afterlife becomes just LittleChina after reloading. This affects all specific locations, not just apartments.

If you save right next to a door, the mod might misread V's position on load. Save a step or two away from entrances to avoid this. The correct outfit will kick in as soon as you walk out and back in.

docs.title: Ripperdoc Fast-Forward
docs.text: Skipping the ripperdoc animation may occasionally cause the outfit to not swap correctly. Sitting in the chair again or reloading fixes it.

docs.title: Heavy Hearts Not Detected
docs.text: Heavy Hearts doesn't have a location name in the game's data, so the club keyword can't detect it. All other nightlife venues work fine.

docs.title: Custom Apartments Need a Scene Transition
docs.text: Custom apartments are detected when the game transitions to a restricted scene near the door coordinates. Open-air locations without this transition (like camps or tents) can't be detected. The mod doesn't check V's position constantly — it only reacts to game events.

docs.title: Pinning During Combat
docs.text: If you equip a pinned (!) outfit during a fight or in a hostile area, the pin won't be recognized until combat ends. Re-equip the pinned outfit afterward to lock it again.

docs.title: Duplicate Outfit Items
docs.text: If two Equipment-EX outfits use the exact same items, the game reports both as "equipped" at the same time. This can confuse the mod's tracking. Avoid creating outfits with identical item combinations.

docs.title: Config Files Overwritten on Update
docs.text: CustomApartments.reds and ContextMap.reds are user-editable config files. Back them up before updating — new versions will overwrite them.

docs.title: Outfit Keywords
docs.text: Name your Equipment-EX outfits with the right keywords and Dynamic Wardrobe knows what to do with them.

docs.title: Activity Keywords
docs.text:
These keywords tell the mod when to use an outfit. The outfit name must start with one of these:

Keyword When It's Used Examples home At any supported apartment home cozy, home towel outdoor Everywhere else (the default) outdoor streetwear, outdoor casual nude Shower and sleep scenes nude ripper Sitting in a ripperdoc chair ripper casual combat During fights and in hostile areas combat, combat street ! (prefix) Locks the outfit — blocks all auto-swaps !tactical stealth, !casual friday

Extra words after the keyword are just labels for you — home cozy and home towel both count as home outfits. Got more than one? The mod picks one at random.

docs.title: Region Keywords
docs.text:
Instead of naming outfits after every individual location, you can use broad region keywords. If an outfit has multiple region keywords, they all need to match.

Keyword Where It Applies corpo Corpo Plaza, City Center, Downtown, North Oaks, Charter Hill, Arasaka Waterfront, Japantown street Glen, Wellsprings, Arroyo, Little China, Kabuki, Northside, Rancho Coronado, Vista Del Rey wild Badlands, South Badlands, North Badlands, Pacifica, Coastview, West Wind Estate, Dogtown club Afterlife, Riot Club, Lizzie's Bar, Clouds, Dark Matter, Totentaz, Embers, El Coyote Cojo

Stacking keywords makes them more specific: corpo club vip only kicks in at clubs in corpo areas (like Clouds in Japantown). More keywords = higher priority.

{% hint style="info" %} Custom mappings — You can customize which locations belong to which region — edit Core/ContextMap.reds to remap areas or create entirely new keywords.

docs.title: Location Keywords
docs.text:
For exact control over a specific spot:

  1. Press F10 in-game to see the current location name (e.g. Badlands_RedPeaks)
  2. Name your outfit using any word from that name (e.g. badlands casual)
  3. The mod is flexible — badlands in your outfit name will match southbadlands in the location too
  4. Use separators to share one outfit across multiple locations: badlands/coastview works in both
  5. A specific location match always beats a region keyword match

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Outfit Name What Happens home cozy Worn at any apartment outdoor streetwear Worn everywhere else nude Worn during shower scenes ripper casual Worn at the ripperdoc corpo formal Worn in upscale corporate areas street Worn in gritty neighborhoods wild tactical Worn in Badlands, Pacifica, Dogtown, etc. club fancy Worn at nightlife venues corpo club vip Worn only at clubs in corpo areas badlands/coastview Worn in Badlands or Coastview combat Worn in any fight combat street Worn in fights in street areas !tactical stealth Locked — stays on no matter what

docs.title: Pinned Outfits
docs.text: Prefix any Equipment-EX outfit name with ! to lock it in place. Dynamic Wardrobe will stop all automatic swapping while that outfit is equipped.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Equip a !-prefixed outfit via Equipment-EX and the mod stops swapping. That's it.

  • !tactical stealth — V stays in this outfit no matter where you go
  • !my favorite look — location changes, combat, hostile areas — all ignored

docs.title: What Gets Blocked
docs.text:
• Location-based outfit changes
  • Region keyword matching
  • Combat and danger zone swaps
  • Outfit restore after combat ends
  • Outfit restore on save/load

docs.title: What Still Works Normally
docs.text:
Scenes that naturally change V's appearance still play out:

  • Home outfit when entering an apartment
  • Ripper outfit at the ripperdoc
  • Nude during shower/sleep
  • All scene restores

These use up the pin — V's outfit changes, the ! name is no longer equipped, and the mod goes back to normal swapping.

docs.title: Unpinning
docs.text:
Equip any non-! outfit via Equipment-EX and the mod takes over again.

To re-pin after a scene uses it up (e.g. shower → nude → home), just re-equip the ! outfit.

docs.title: Matching Exclusion
docs.text: An outfit named !home cozy will never be automatically picked as a "home" outfit. The ! prefix tells the mod to ignore it entirely during matching — it only activates when you manually equip it via Equipment-EX.

docs.title: Save/Load
docs.text:
Pinned outfits survive saving and loading. Equipment-EX restores the outfit visually, and Dynamic Wardrobe recognizes the pin as soon as the game loads.

{% hint style="warning" %} Known Limitation — If you equip a pinned outfit during a fight or while in a hostile area, the pin won't kick in until the fight or zone ends. The mod pauses outfit tracking during combat to make sure it can restore the right outfit afterward. Just re-equip the pinned outfit once you're out of combat.

docs.title: Priority System
docs.text: When V moves to a new location, Dynamic Wardrobe picks the best outfit using a simple order. Higher priority always wins.

docs.title: 1. Exact Location Match (highest priority)
docs.text:
If you have an outfit named after the specific location, it wins.

afterlife at the Afterlife beats everything else.

docs.title: 2. Region Keyword Match
docs.text:
If there's no exact match, the mod checks region keywords (corpo, street, wild, club).

club casual applies at any club when there's no specific outfit for that venue.

More keywords = more specific = higher priority.

corpo club beats club at a corpo club.

docs.title: 3. outdoor (lowest priority)
docs.text: If nothing else matches, V wears the outdoor outfit.

docs.title: Special Contexts
docs.text:
These take over when they're active, regardless of location:

Context When It's Active home V is inside a supported apartment combat V is in a fight or hostile area (also supports region matching) nude The game undresses V (shower, romance scenes) ripper V is sitting in a ripperdoc chair pinned (!) A ! outfit is equipped — all auto-swaps are blocked

docs.title: How Matching Works
docs.text:
Outfit names are split into words by any separator (spaces, slashes, dashes, etc.). Each word is checked against the current location name — the mod is flexible, so badlands in an outfit name matches southbadlands in the location, and vice versa.

When multiple outfits match, one is picked at random. So having home cozy, home towel, and home lazy gives V a different look on each apartment visit.

docs.title: Separators Between Locations = "Either One"
docs.text: badlands/coastview applies in either Badlands or Coastview.

docs.title: Multiple Region Keywords = "Both Must Match"
docs.text: corpo club vip only applies where the location is both corpo and club (e.g. Clouds, Embers).

docs.title: Visual Summary
docs.text:
V enters a new location
│
├─ Wearing a pinned (!) outfit?
│  └─ Yes → skip all swaps
│
├─ In combat or a hostile area?
│  └─ Yes → combat outfit
│
├─ Inside an apartment?
│  └─ Yes → home outfit
│
├─ Exact location match?
│  └─ Yes → use it
│
├─ Region keyword match?
│  └─ Yes → use best match (most keywords)
│
└─ Nothing matches → outdoor outfit

docs.title: Step 1: Create Your Outfits
docs.text:
Open Equipment-EX and save outfits with these names:

  • home — what V wears at the apartment
  • outdoor — V's default look everywhere else
  • nude — applied when the game undresses V (shower, romance scenes)
  • combat — gear for fights and hostile areas
  • ripper — what V wears at the ripperdoc (requires Immersive Rippers)

That's it! Walk into your apartment and the mod takes over.

docs.title: Step 2: Add Location Outfits
docs.text:
Press F10 anywhere in the game to see your current location name. Name an outfit after that location and it'll apply there automatically.

  • afterlife — applies at the Afterlife
  • japantown — applies anywhere in Japantown
  • badlands — applies in the Badlands

Got more than one favorite spot that should share the same look? Name an outfit after both locations — e.g. afterlife/clouds — and it'll apply at either one.

Or use region keywords to cover whole groups of locations at once:

Keyword Covers corpo Upscale areas — Corpo Plaza, City Center, North Oaks, Japantown... street Gritty neighborhoods — Kabuki, Little China, Arroyo, Glen... wild Rough territory — Badlands, Pacifica, Dogtown... club Nightlife venues — Afterlife, Clouds, Lizzie's Bar, Totentaz...

See Outfit Keywords for the full list, or check out the Examples page to see how it all comes together.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Extra words after keywords are just for you — outdoor streetwear and outdoor casual both count as outdoor outfits
  • Combine region keywords for more specific matching — corpo club vip only applies at clubs in upscale areas
  • Use separators to match multiple locations with one outfit — badlands/coastview works in both areas
  • Press F10 anywhere to see your current location name
  • All region mappings are customizable — edit ContextMap.reds to add locations or create new keywords

docs.title: What Persists in Your Save
docs.text:
Dynamic Wardrobe writes two quest facts to your save file:

Data Type Purpose dw_is_at_home Quest fact (0, 1, or 2) Tracks whether V was inside an apartment when you saved. 0 = no, 1 = vanilla apartment, 2 = custom apartment dw_block_location Quest fact (0 or 1) Suppresses outfit changes during scripted scenes (cutscenes, braindances). Automatically cleared when gameplay resumes

Three possible values total.

docs.title: What Does NOT Persist
docs.text:
• Outfit selections (managed entirely by Equipment-EX)
  • Location state, custom location flags, ripper state
  • Cached outfit names, block flags, callback handles
  • All runtime state is cleared on game exit

docs.title: Is It Safe to Uninstall?
docs.text:
Yes. The only persistent data are two quest facts, both harmless integer flags with no vanilla game meaning. The game engine ignores unknown quest facts entirely.

You can safely remove Dynamic Wardrobe at any time without corrupting your save or causing errors.

Equipment-EX outfits are managed by EX itself — Dynamic Wardrobe only reads and loads them, never creates or modifies outfit data.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
• v1.4 — Dogtown Hideout, River's Apartment, Custom Apartments, Pinned Outfits
  • v1.3 — Combat Outfits, Scene Tier Guard, Internal Refactoring
  • v1.2.1 — Manual Outfit Change Detection Fix
  • v1.2 — Regional Context Keywords
  • v1.1 — Judy's Apartment, Quest Outfit Guard
  • v1.0 — Initial Release

docs.title: Initial Release
docs.text:
• Context-driven outfit swapping — automatically changes Equipment-EX outfits based on game context
  • Apartment detection — proximity-based entrance detection with enum validation for vanilla apartments (H10, Glen, Northside, Japantown, Corpo Plaza)
  • Home/outdoor outfit cycle — applies "home" outfit on entry; on exit, matches location, context, or falls back to "outdoor"
  • Scene handling — intercepts QuestDisable/QuestRestore wardrobe requests to swap nude/home outfits during shower and sleep scenes
  • Ripperdoc support — detects ripperdoc workspots via Tier4 entry, applies "ripper" outfit, restores previous on exit (designed for use with Immersive Rippers and Immersive Rippers - Dogtown — vanilla game never uses ripperdoc chairs as active workspots)
  • Drive scene handling — intercepts UnequipWardrobeSet (EX no-op) to swap outfits during romance drive scenes (designed for use with Hangout Romances — the drive scene trigger does not exist in vanilla)
  • Location matching — bidirectional keyword matching between outfit names and location enums with right-to-left priority
  • Multi-location outfits — use separators (e.g. badlands/coastview) to match multiple areas with a single outfit
  • Random selection — multiple outfits matching the same keyword are picked at random
  • Unequip blocking — prevents engine from stripping equipment during Tier4 scene transitions at home
  • Persistent home state — saves dw_is_at_home quest fact so home context survives save/load
  • Shower deferral setting — optional mode where home outfit applies only after the game naturally undresses V
  • F10 location display — configurable keybind to show current location enum name on screen
  • Master toggle — enable/disable all mod features from Mod Settings

docs.title: Localization
docs.text:
• 19 languages — full Codeware localization via ModLocalizationProvider
  • Arabic, Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional), Czech, English, French, German, Hungarian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Polish, Portuguese (Brazilian), Russian, Spanish, Spanish (Mexican), Thai, Turkish, Ukrainian

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• Event-driven architecture — blackboard listener on UI_Map.currentLocationEnumName, no polling
  • Equipment-EX integration via OutfitSystem.GetInstance() — read-only (never creates or modifies outfit data)
  • Single quest fact persistence (dw_is_at_home) — safe to uninstall at any time
  • Guard against double listener registration in OnGameAttached
  • m_wardrobeBlockUnequip cleared by both WorkspotFinished and QuestRestore (prevents permanent block on abnormal scene exit)
  • Cached nude outfit CName on apartment entry for instant scene loading

docs.title: File Changes
docs.text: Action File Added r6/input/DynamicWardrobeInput.xml (Input Loader keybind definitions) Added Core/Fields.reds (@addField declarations on PlayerPuppet) Added Core/OutfitManager.reds (EX integration, outfit matching, location matching) Added Core/Settings.reds (ScriptableSystem with categorized @runtimeProperty settings) Added Events/HomeDetection.reds (apartment detection, location callback, home state) Added Events/InputHandler.reds (F10 location display keybind) Added Events/SceneRestore.reds (QuestDisable/QuestRestore/UnequipWardrobeSet/OnUnequipRequest wraps) Added Events/WorkspotHandler.reds (Tier4 entry, ripperdoc detection, WorkspotFinished) Added Localization/Provider.reds (ModLocalizationProvider with 19 languages) Added Localization/Packages/*.reds (19 language translation files)

docs.title: New Feature: Judy's Apartment Support
docs.text:
• Judy's apartment (Kabuki_JudysApartment) is now recognized as a home location
  • Quest-gated — only activates after completing Pyramid Song (romance or friendship path)
  • Full home lifecycle — entrance detection, home/nude/outdoor outfit swapping, save/load persistence
  • Kabuki district fallback — prevents false home exits on save/load

docs.title: Bug Fix: Quest Outfit Guard
docs.text:
• Quest outfit protection — Dynamic Wardrobe no longer swaps outfits when the game has equipped a quest outfit (e.g. tactical diving suit)
  • Uses Equipment-EX's IsBlocked() check — detects items with the UnequipBlocked tag in the Outfit slot
  • All four outfit loading paths guarded — ApplyOutfitByPrefix, ReapplyOrRandomize, ApplyOutfitByLocation, and WardrobeLeaveHome

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• Judy's apartment gated by quest facts — sq030_judy_lover >= 1 || sq030_friendship >= 1 || sq030_v_got_apartment >= 1 (covers romance, friendship, and edge case paths)
  • IsVanillaApartment renamed to IsKnownApartment — now accepts player param for quest system access
  • IsBlocked() check — reuses Equipment-EX's built-in guard that detects the UnequipBlocked tag on items in the Outfit slot

docs.title: File Changes
docs.text: Action File Modified Core/OutfitManager.reds (added IsBlocked() guard to all outfit loading methods) Modified Events/HomeDetection.reds (added Judy's apartment detection, Kabuki district fallback, entrance coords)

docs.title: Bug Fix: Manual Outfit Change Detection
docs.text:
• Dedup guard now checks equipped state — if you manually change your outfit via the Equipment-EX menu, the mod correctly detects the change and re-applies the appropriate context or location outfit when you move to a new area
  • Previously, manually swapping outfits could cause the mod to skip re-applying because it thought the same context was still active

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• ApplyOutfitByLocation and ApplyContextOutfit dedup guards now verify outfitSystem.IsEquipped(player.m_wardrobeLastOutfit) in addition to comparing m_wardrobeLastContext — if the tracked outfit is no longer equipped (manual EX change), the guard fails and the method re-applies
  • ApplyOutfitByPrefix intentionally unchanged — some prefix contexts (home, nude, ripper) are used during scene transitions where the engine can temporarily strip items; adding IsEquipped there would cause false failures and spurious re-applies mid-scene

docs.title: File Changes
docs.text: Action File Modified Core/OutfitManager.reds (added IsEquipped check to dedup guards in ApplyOutfitByLocation and ApplyContextOutfit)

docs.title: New Feature: Regional Context Keywords
docs.text:
• Four context keywords — corpo, street, wild, club — let you assign outfits to broad categories of locations instead of naming every individual area
  • corpo covers upscale areas: CorpoPlaza, CityCenter, Downtown, NorthOaks, CharterHill, ArasakaWaterfront, JapanTown
  • street covers gritty neighborhoods: Glen, Wellsprings, Arroyo, LittleChina, Kabuki, Northside, RanchoCoronado, VistaDelRey
  • wild covers dangerous zones: Badlands, SouthBadlands, NorthBadlands, Pacifica, Coastview, WestWindEstate, Dogtown
  • club covers nightlife venues: Afterlife, RiotClub, LizziesBar, Clouds, DarkMatter, Totentaz, Embers, ElCoyoteCojo (Heavy Hearts has no location enum and cannot be detected)
  • Multi-keyword scoring — outfit named corpo club formal only applies at clubs in corpo areas (both keywords must match). Higher keyword count = higher priority
  • Customizable mappings — edit Core/ContextMap.reds to add locations, remap areas, or create entirely new context keywords
  • Fallback chain — specific location match → context keyword match → "outdoor" prefix

docs.title: Bug Fixes
docs.text: • Entrance detection radius tightened from 5.0 to 1.0 units — reduces the chance of false home exits when loading a save near apartment doors

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• New fallback chain in location callback — HasOutfitForLocation → HasContextOutfit (scored, all-must-match) → "outdoor" prefix
  • Context keywords excluded from location matching — words like "corpo", "street", "wild", "club" in outfit names are filtered out during location word matching to prevent false positives
  • Removed pre-home outfit caching — leaving an apartment now runs the normal fallback chain (location → context → outdoor) instead of restoring the previously worn outfit
  • m_wardrobeReady flag — guards location callback against firing before OnGameAttached finishes initializing
  • Mod-disabled safety — disabling the mod now only resets in-memory state (no longer clears the persisted quest fact, avoiding race conditions with ModSettings initialization)

docs.title: File Changes
docs.text: Action File Added Core/ContextMap.reds (location → context keyword mappings, user-customizable) Modified Core/Fields.reds (added m_wardrobeReady flag, removed m_wardrobePreHomeOutfit/m_wardrobePreHomeContext) Modified Core/OutfitManager.reds (added context scoring, SplitOutfitKeywordsForLocation, GetContextOutfits, HasContextOutfit, ApplyContextOutfit) Modified Events/HomeDetection.reds (context fallback in location callback and WardrobeLeaveHome, tightened entrance radius, removed pre-home restore, m_wardrobeReady guard)

docs.title: New Feature: Combat Outfits
docs.text:
Automatically swap to a combat outfit when V enters combat or a dangerous zone (shoot-on-sight areas), and restore the previous outfit when leaving.

  • Basic usage — name an Equipment-EX outfit with the keyword combat (e.g. "combat", "combat gear") and it applies everywhere during combat or dangerous zones
  • Context-aware combat — combine combat with context keywords for area-specific combat gear:
  • combat street → only in street areas
  • combat wild → only in wild/badlands areas
  • combat street club → only at street clubs (e.g. Afterlife) — highest context score wins
  • Location-aware combat — combine combat with location keywords:
  • combat badlands → only in Badlands
  • combat japantown → only in Japantown
  • Dangerous zones — entering a hostile area (e.g. gang hideouts, enemy territory) triggers combat outfits automatically, even before combat starts. If V enters actual combat while in a dangerous zone, the outfit stays. On zone exit, the pre-zone outfit is restored
  • Priority: combat + location match → combat + context match → plain combat fallback
  • Restoration: on combat/zone exit, the pre-combat outfit is restored. If invalid, the normal location/context/outdoor fallback chain runs
  • Guards: combat outfits are skipped at home and during ripperdoc sessions. Location/context outfit changes are suppressed while in combat or dangerous zones
  • Isolation: combat outfits never leak into normal outfit matching, and normal outfits never appear during combat resolution

docs.title: Bug Fix: Scene Tier Location Guard
docs.text:
Dynamic Wardrobe now blocks all location-based outfit changes during high scene tiers (Tier3+), preventing unwanted outfit swaps during braindances, cutscenes, ripperdoc sessions, showers, and any other scripted sequence. Tier2 (walkable scripted areas like shops and clinics) remains unblocked so location outfits can apply naturally.

  • Problem — braindances and other scene transitions involve loading screens that wipe all runtime @addField values. Any runtime flag (like "block location changes") gets reset mid-scene, causing the location callback and OnGameAttached to fire freely — swapping V's outfit mid-scene
  • Root cause fix — uses a persisted quest fact (dw_block_location) instead of runtime fields. The OnSceneTierChange callback sets the flag when the game enters Tier3+ (BDs, cutscenes, ripperdoc, showers) and clears it when V returns to Tier1–2 (full gameplay or walkable scripted areas). Tier0 (undefined/uninitialized) is ignored to prevent mid-scene loading screens from clearing the block. The quest fact survives loading screens that wipe runtime state
  • Universal coverage — instead of handling each scene type individually (braindance, ripperdoc, shower, etc.), a single tier-based guard catches all of them. The location callback, OnGameAttached, and WardrobeLeaveHome all check this flag and skip outfit changes when active

docs.title: Internal Refactoring & Edge Case Fixes
docs.text:
A cleanup pass across the entire codebase — more robust and maintainable internals.

Redundancy Removal: * Extracted ApplyLocationOrFallback helper — the location → context → outdoor fallback chain was duplicated in OnWardrobeLocationChanged and WardrobeLeaveHome. Now a single shared method in OutfitManager * Extracted GuardedHomeSync and LoadNudePreserveTracking helpers — the guarded sync + nude loading pattern was duplicated across UnequipWardrobeSet and OnQuestDisableWardrobeSetRequest. Now two reusable methods in OutfitManager * Extracted PickRandom helper — the random outfit selection pattern was repeated in three Apply* methods * Removed redundant RefreshOutfitList calls — HasOutfitForLocation and ApplyContextOutfit no longer refresh the outfit list individually; ApplyLocationOrFallback refreshes once at the top for the entire chain

Edge Case Fixes: * Ripper flag stuck on fast travel — if V fast-travels while in a ripperdoc workspot, OnWorkspotFinishedEvent may never fire, leaving m_wardrobeInRipperWorkspot stuck. The flag is now cleared on any location change (safe because ripper workspots don't trigger location changes) * Missing m_wardrobeReady guard on hangout callback — OnWardrobeHangoutEnded could theoretically fire during initialization before the mod is ready. Now guarded * WardrobeLeaveHome missing location block guard — WardrobeLeaveHome now checks IsLocationBlocked before applying outdoor outfit, preventing stale home-exit logic from firing during scene transitions * Ripper outfit corrupts tracking — during ripperdoc sessions, game events (e.g. UnequipWardrobeSet) call SyncCurrentOutfit, which would pick up the temporary ripper outfit and overwrite the pre-ripper tracking. On workspot exit, the mod would try to "restore" the ripper outfit instead of the actual previous outfit. Fixed by guarding SyncCurrentOutfit to skip when m_wardrobeInRipperWorkspot is true * Stale location block on save/load — OnGameAttached now checks the current PSM tier and clears dw_block_location only on explicit Tier1 (== 1), not Tier0 (== 0, undefined/uninitialized). Mid-scene loading screens trigger OnGameAttached with PSM not yet set up (tier 0), so using <= 1 would incorrectly clear the block during BDs and cutscenes * Missing IsBlocked() guard on ripperdoc restore — OnWorkspotFinishedEvent now checks outfitSystem.IsBlocked() before restoring the pre-ripper outfit, preventing quest outfit override on workspot exit

Code Cleanup: * Removed dead m_wardrobeInCustomLocation field — leftover from an earlier design that was never cleaned up. Removed from Fields.reds and all assignment sites * Inverted empty if-block in GetContextOutfits — replaced if bad { } else if good { ... } with if good { ... } for readability * Dynamic context key builder — ApplyContextOutfit no longer hardcodes keyword order (club, corpo, street, wild). Now sorts the contexts array via bubble sort, making it future-proof for user-defined custom keywords in ContextMap.reds * Removed redundant IsInBraindance helper — braindance suppression is now fully handled by the persisted dw_block_location scene tier guard. Combat and zone handlers use IsLocationBlocked instead, which covers all Tier3+ scenes (not just BDs)

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• ApplyCombatOutfit — temporarily swaps m_wardrobeOutfitList to combat-only, then reuses the existing location → context fallback chain. Zero duplicated matching logic. Falls back to any combat outfit if no location/context match. Used by both combat entry and dangerous zone entry
  • Dangerous zone detection — wraps the game's existing OnZoneChange callback on PlayerPuppet, which receives SecurityAreaData via a Variant listener on PlayerStateMachine.SecurityZoneData. ESecurityAreaType.DANGEROUS (value 3) triggers the combat outfit. m_wardrobeInDangerousZone flag tracks zone state separately from m_wardrobeInCombat so combat exit knows whether to restore
  • Scene tier location guard — OnSceneTierChange wraps the game's existing PSM callback on PlayerPuppet. Tier3+ sets the persisted dw_block_location quest fact (survives loading screens that wipe runtime @addField values). Tier1–2 clears it (Tier0 is ignored — uninitialized PSM during mid-scene loads). The location callback, combat/zone handlers, OnGameAttached, and WardrobeLeaveHome all check this flag and skip outfit changes when active. Tier2 (walkable scripted areas like shops/clinics) is intentionally unblocked so location outfits apply naturally on entry
  • ApplyLocationOrFallback — now filters out combat outfits before running the chain, preventing "combat street" from matching as a regular "street" outfit. Single entry point for the fallback chain: RefreshOutfitList → HasOutfitForLocation → ApplyOutfitByLocation / HasContextOutfit → ApplyContextOutfit / ApplyOutfitByPrefix("outdoor"). Called by both OnWardrobeLocationChanged and WardrobeLeaveHome
  • GuardedHomeSync — wraps SyncCurrentOutfit with a home-only filter: syncs, checks IsWearingPrefix("home"), reverts tracking if the synced outfit isn't a home outfit. Prevents scene-triggered syncs from picking up nude/non-home outfits
  • LoadNudePreserveTracking — saves m_wardrobeLastOutfit/m_wardrobeLastContext, loads nude (cached CName or by prefix), then restores tracking. Used by both UnequipWardrobeSet (drive scene) and OnQuestDisableWardrobeSetRequest (shower/sleep)
  • Ripper sync guard — SyncCurrentOutfit returns immediately when m_wardrobeInRipperWorkspot is true, preventing game events during the ripperdoc session from overwriting the pre-ripper outfit tracking
  • Ripper flag reset — m_wardrobeInRipperWorkspot cleared at the top of OnWardrobeLocationChanged, before any outfit logic runs. Location changes always fire on fast travel, so the flag cannot get stuck
  • Context key sort — ApplyContextOutfit copies the contexts array, bubble-sorts it alphabetically via StrCmp, then joins with + and prefixes with ctx:. Custom keywords added to ContextMap.reds are automatically included in the dedup key

docs.title: File Changes
docs.text: Action File Added Events/CombatHandler.reds (wraps OnCombatStateChanged + OnZoneChange — combat/danger zone entry/exit with save/restore tracking; IsLocationBlocked guard on both handlers) Modified Core/OutfitManager.reds (added ApplyCombatOutfit, PickRandom, GuardedHomeSync, LoadNudePreserveTracking, ApplyLocationOrFallback; ripper sync guard in SyncCurrentOutfit; combat outfit isolation in normal flow; removed redundant refreshes; dynamic context key sort; inverted empty if-block) Modified Core/Fields.reds (added m_wardrobeInCombat, m_wardrobeInDangerousZone, m_wardrobeReady; removed m_wardrobeInCustomLocation) Modified Events/HomeDetection.reds (replaced duplicated fallback chains with ApplyLocationOrFallback; added combat guard on location changes; added SetLocationBlocked, IsLocationBlocked helpers to HomeDetection class; added dw_block_location guard on location callback and OnGameAttached; stale location block safety clear on load; WardrobeLeaveHome guarded by IsLocationBlocked; added ripper flag reset on location change; removed dead flag assignments; removed redundant IsInBraindance helper) Modified Events/WorkspotHandler.reds (added OnSceneTierChange — sets dw_block_location on Tier3+ entry, clears on Tier1–2 return, ignores Tier0; removed location block from Tier4 OnEnter (now handled by tier change); added IsBlocked() guard to ripperdoc restore) Modified Events/SceneRestore.reds (replaced duplicated sync+nude pattern with GuardedHomeSync + LoadNudePreserveTracking; added m_wardrobeReady guard to hangout callback)

docs.title: New Feature: Dogtown Hideout (Kress Street) Support
docs.text:
• Phantom Liberty's Dogtown apartment (Dogtown_Hideout) is now recognized as a home location
  • Uses the same detection as vanilla apartments — unique enum via the location callback, no configuration needed
  • The engine only reports Dogtown_Hideout after the apartment is unlocked (quest-gated trigger volume), so no script-side quest fact check is required
  • Dogtown added to IsApartmentDistrict for save/load fallback

docs.title: New Feature: River's Apartment (Trailer Park) Support
docs.text:
• River's apartment in the Badlands is now supported as a pre-configured custom apartment — no setup needed
  • Quest-gated — only activates after completing "Following the River" (romance or friendship path)
  • Uses OR gate logic: sq029_river_lover (romance) OR sq029_done / sq029_stash (friendship) — any path unlocks it
  • hasShower = true — the apartment has a usable shower, so the shower deferral setting is respected
  • Ships as a built-in entry in CustomApartments.reds and doubles as a practical example for users adding their own apartments

docs.title: New Feature: Custom Apartment Support
docs.text:
Dynamic Wardrobe now supports modded apartments. Register any apartment by adding its entrance coordinates to Core/CustomApartments.reds — the mod handles the rest. River's apartment is included out of the box.

  • Simple config API — DWApartment.Add(apartments, hasShower).Entrance(x, y, z) with chained builder syntax
  • Multiple entrances — chain .Entrance() calls for apartments with more than one door
  • Quest gates — chain .Gate("fact_name", minValue) calls for story-locked apartments (AND logic — all must pass), or .OrGate() for apartments unlockable via multiple quest paths (OR logic — at least one must pass)
  • Shower deferral — hasShower = true respects the shower deferral setting; hasShower = false applies home outfit immediately on entry
  • Event-driven detection — uses SceneTier2 transitions + entrance proximity (no polling)
  • Persisted home state — dw_is_at_home now stores 2 for custom apartments (vs 1 for vanilla), enabling correct exit detection after save/load
  • Pre-configured — River's apartment (quest-gated with OR logic) ships ready to use
  • In-game helpers — CET console commands for coordinates and quest facts documented in the config file

docs.title: New Feature: Pinned Outfits (! Prefix)
docs.text:
Prefix any Equipment-EX outfit name with ! to pin it — Dynamic Wardrobe will skip all automatic location, context, combat, and save/load outfit swaps while the pinned outfit is equipped.

  • Stateless and disposable — no new settings, fields, or persisted data. The pin is detected by checking the currently equipped outfit name
  • Blocks — location changes, context matching, outdoor fallback, combat/danger zone entry, combat/zone exit restore, and home outfit restore on save/load
  • Plays normally — home outfit on apartment entry, ripper outfit on ripperdoc chair, nude during shower/sleep, and all scene restores. These consume the pin naturally (V's outfit changes, the ! name is no longer equipped)
  • Matching exclusion — ! outfits are filtered from all automatic matching pools. !home cozy will never be selected as a "home" outfit by the mod
  • Survives save/load — Equipment-EX restores the ! outfit visually, and DW syncs its tracking on load to detect the pin before any outfit logic runs
  • Re-equip to re-pin — after a scene consumes the pin (e.g. shower → nude → home restore), simply re-equip the ! outfit via EX to re-pin

docs.title: Bug Fix: Corpo Plaza Apartment Enum Mismatch
docs.text:
• Problem: Inside the Corpo Plaza apartment, the game can change the location enum to CityCenter or Downtown instead of the expected CorpoPlaza. This caused the mod's safety fallback to think V had left the apartment, triggering a location/context outfit swap (e.g. applying a "corpo" outfit) instead of keeping the home outfit.
  • Fix: Added CityCenter and Downtown to IsApartmentDistrict so the safety fallback correctly recognizes V is still inside the Corpo Plaza apartment regardless of which enum the game reports.

docs.title: Bug Fix: Runtime Outfit Tracking Sync
docs.text:
• Problem: When V manually equipped an outfit via Equipment-EX, Dynamic Wardrobe's internal tracking didn't update until a game reload. This caused location/context outfits to override manual selections on the next location change.
  • Fix: Added SyncCurrentOutfit (with combat guard) at the top of the location change callback. DW now syncs its tracking with EX's actual equipped outfit on every location change, catching manual outfit changes and refreshing the cached outfit list. In normal operation this adds just one lightweight IsEquipped check (early return when tracking is already correct).

docs.title: User Config Files
docs.text: Core/CustomApartments.reds and Core/ContextMap.reds are user-editable config files. Back up these files before updating — new versions will overwrite them.

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• OutfitManager.IsPinned — checks if m_wardrobeLastOutfit starts with ! via StrBeginsWith. No new fields or persisted facts
  • Pin guards — ApplyLocationOrFallback, ApplyCombatOutfit, combat/zone exit restore blocks, OnWardrobeLocationChanged, and OnGameAttached home restore all check IsPinned before loading outfits. State transitions (flags, facts) run untouched
  • Matching pool filter — GetOutfitsByPrefix, ApplyOutfitByLocation, and GetContextOutfits skip outfits starting with ! so they can never be autonomously selected
  • Save/load sync — OnGameAttached calls SyncCurrentOutfit before any pin checks to populate m_wardrobeLastOutfit from EX's restored outfit (runtime @addField values are wiped on load)
  • Location change sync — OnWardrobeLocationChanged calls SyncCurrentOutfit (combat-guarded) before any logic to catch manual EX outfit changes and refresh the cached outfit list at runtime
  • OnSceneTierChange Tier2 entry — when SceneTier changes to 2 near a custom apartment entrance, WardrobeEnterHome fires. Tier1 return while dw_is_at_home == 2 triggers WardrobeLeaveHome
  • WardrobePersistHome — now takes Int32 instead of Bool: 0 = not home, 1 = vanilla apartment, 2 = custom apartment. Exit detection for custom apartments checks the persisted fact value instead of the runtime m_wardrobeCurrentCustomApt ref (which is wiped on load)
  • DWApartmentDef — data class with entrance coordinates, hasShower flag, and quest gates (AND + OR). Builder methods return ref<DWApartmentDef> for chaining
  • HomeDetection.IsNearCustomApartment — iterates custom apartment configs, checks entrance proximity (1.0 unit) and quest gates (both AND and OR), returns matching def or null
  • Safety fallback skip — location callback safety fallback (no Tier2, unknown district → leave home) is skipped for custom apartments (dw_is_at_home == 2) since save/load may report unrelated location enums

docs.title: File Changes
docs.text: Action File Added Core/CustomApartments.reds (user-editable custom apartment config with documented examples and CET helper commands) Added Core/DWApartmentDef.reds (apartment definition data class with DWQuestGate struct and DWApartment helper) Modified Core/Fields.reds (added m_wardrobeCurrentCustomApt: ref<DWApartmentDef>) Modified Events/HomeDetection.reds (added CityCenter and Downtown to IsApartmentDistrict for Corpo Plaza enum mismatch fix; combat-guarded SyncCurrentOutfit in location callback for runtime tracking sync; added Dogtown to IsKnownApartment and IsApartmentDistrict; extended IsNearApartmentEntrance for custom apartments; added IsNearCustomApartment; WardrobePersistHome now Int32; WardrobeEnterHome persists 2 for custom; hasShower deferral override; safety fallback skip for custom apartments; pin guard in OnGameAttached and OnWardrobeLocationChanged; early SyncCurrentOutfit on load for pin detection) Modified Core/OutfitManager.reds (added IsPinned helper; pin guard in ApplyLocationOrFallback and ApplyCombatOutfit; ! outfit filter in GetOutfitsByPrefix, ApplyOutfitByLocation, GetContextOutfits) Modified Events/CombatHandler.reds (pin guard on combat exit and zone exit restore blocks) Modified Events/WorkspotHandler.reds (added Tier2 custom apartment detection + Tier1 exit in OnSceneTierChange)
